images.caption: Figure 1:Motivation and high-level finding of MMG2Skill-Bench and MMG2Skill.Public multimodal guides contain useful procedural knowledge for long-horizon agents, but raw guide prompting can hurt performance, motivating guide-to-skill learning.
Figure 2:MMG2Skill-Bench composition.Task-family counts and guide coverage by domain.

Figure 3:MMG2Skill framework.The figure shows the closed-loop pipeline that constructs guide-derived skills, uses them during rollout, and revises the skill cache from trajectory diagnoses across attempts.

Figure 4:Revision dynamics across attemptsKK.Lines show per-attempt mean score (%, left axis) for GPT-5.5 and Qwen3.6-Plus on the three domains. Bars show cumulativelikely_successtrigger rate (right axis).

Figure 5:Policy gap between early-stop and full-run deployment.Per-domain mean score (%) at each attemptKK, averaged across the model set. Bars contrast the full-run view (attemptKK) with the early-stop view (§4.1).

Figure 6:MMG2Skill failure attribution by pipeline component.Each panel shows failed MMG2Skill cases in one domain, stacked by component and error-code category across the four backbones. Agent-loop skill-follow errors are placed last within the agent-loop taxonomy, and non-error analyzer or refiner tags are excluded.

header: Appendix CBenchmark and Guide Corpus Details
Appendix DExtended Results and Robustness Checks

images.caption: Figure 7:Per-(model, domain) revision dynamics.This expanded view of Figure4shows one panel per (model, domain) pair. Each panel reports absolute mean full-run score (dashed, filled squares), early-stop score (solid, open circles), and the cumulativelikely_successtrigger rate as a faded right-axis bar.

Figure 8:Extended revision horizon(N=7N{=}7) on Qwen3.6-Plus for the GUI and Game domains. Panels report per-attempt mean score (%) under the full-run and early-stop policies, the cumulativelikely_successtrigger rate on the right axis, and skill character count.

Figure 9:Early-stop skill character count across attemptsNNunder MMG2Skill on the three MMG2Skill-Bench domains. Each curve reports one backbone’s task-mean skill-set size selected by thelikely_successearly-stop rule within budgetNN. Bands show standard deviation.
Figure 10:Direct root-cause prompting diagnostic.Each panel reports cumulative success rate under the same early-stop policy used by MMG2Skill.
Figure 11:Analyzer signal calibration.The figure compareslikely_successandno_issuetrigger rates, early-stop scores, and gaps to full-run across attemptsKK.

Figure 12:Per-task VLM API calls by pipeline stageon the three MMG2Skill-Bench domains (Strategy averages Doudizhu and Mahjong, Hold’em excluded). Analyzer calls are estimated from the appendix chunk sizeCCand each attempt’s decision steps as⌈L/C⌉\lceil L/C\rceil. Refiner calls occur between counted attempts.

Figure 13:Per-task token consumption by pipeline stageon the same three domains and backbones as Figure12, counted with theo200ktiktoken.Fullsums allN=5N{=}5attempts.MMG2Skillstops at the firstlikely_success.
